workflow.purpose:
Standalone code review for a story's implementation. The session executing this workflow IS the code reviewer — it follows the instructions from ace-code-reviewer.md directly.
Loads story context (AC, Technical Solution, coding standards), identifies changed/created files, performs 3-level artifact verification, anti-pattern detection, coding standards enforcement, and tech debt discovery.
This is a read-only workflow — it does NOT modify code, only reports issues.
workflow.agent: This workflow uses the `ace-code-reviewer` agent. The session executing this workflow IS the code reviewer — it follows the agent's review process, checklist, stub detection patterns, dead code detection rules, and report format directly. It does NOT spawn a separate agent.
workflow.mandatory-context: Read all files referenced by the invoking command's execution-context before starting. Also read any document or text passed as parameter ($ARGUMENTS) in the invoking command.

step.name: init-and-validate
step.substep: Parse `$ARGUMENTS` to extract: - `story` (REQUIRED): file path or GitHub URL/issue number

Execute environment detection:
```bash INIT=$(node ~/.claude/agile-context-engineering/src/ace-tools.js init execute-story {story_param}) ```
Parse INIT JSON for: - `has_git`, `has_gh_cli`, `github_project` - `story_source`, `story_valid`, `story_error`, `story_content` - `story` (id, title, status, size), `feature` (id, title), `epic` (id, title) - `has_acceptance_criteria`, `acceptance_criteria_count` - `has_technical_solution`, `has_wiki_refs`, `has_coding_standards` - `paths.*`

substep.name: validate-story
substep.validation-gate: Display `INIT.story_error` and STOP: ``` x {INIT.story_error} Provide a valid story file path or GitHub issue. ```
STOP: ``` x Story has no acceptance criteria. Run /ace:plan-story first to define AC scenarios. ```
STOP: ``` x Story has no technical solution. Run /ace:plan-story first to generate the technical solution. ```

step.name: load-context
step.substep: Read `INIT.paths.story_file` and extract: - **Acceptance Criteria** — Gherkin scenarios (what MUST be implemented) - **Technical Solution** — component breakdown (what files SHOULD exist and what they do) - **Out of Scope** — what should NOT have been built

substep.name: read-coding-standards
substep.variant: Read `INIT.paths.coding_standards` — key rules for compliance checking.

step.substep:
Determine which files to review:
```bash git diff --name-only HEAD git diff --cached --name-only git status --short ``` Use changed files as the review scope.
Use the Technical Solution's artifact list to identify expected files and check their current state in the codebase.
Display: ``` i Review scope: {count} files identified ```

step.name: execute-review
step.substep:
For EACH artifact mentioned in the Technical Solution, perform the 3-level verification as defined in ace-code-reviewer.md:
Verify the file exists at the expected path. ```bash [ -f "path/to/file" ] && echo "FOUND" || echo "MISSING" ```
Read each file. Check for stub patterns defined in ace-code-reviewer.md's stub detection section. A substantive file has real logic, real data access, real rendering — not placeholders, empty handlers, or static returns.
Verify each artifact is imported/referenced AND used by consuming code. Adapt search patterns to the project's language (as documented in ace-code-reviewer.md's Level 3 patterns).
Build the artifact status matrix (VERIFIED / ORPHANED / STUB / MISSING).

Execute the full review checklist from ace-code-reviewer.md:
Dead code — unused imports, unreferenced functions, orphaned files Backwards-compatible shims — re-exports, deprecated wrappers, underscore renames Stub implementations — empty returns, placeholder content, no-op handlers TODO/FIXME/HACK/PLACEHOLDER/XXX comments in new/modified code Hardcoded values — magic numbers, hardcoded URLs, embedded credentials Missing error handling — uncaught exceptions, unchecked responses AC coverage gaps — verify EACH Gherkin scenario is implemented Coding standards violations — mandatory check if standards available
Console/debug logging left behind Missing tests for new functionality Out of scope work — code NOT in the AC
Use grep, file reads, and code analysis as described in ace-code-reviewer.md's dead code detection and stub detection sections.

While reviewing, note pre-existing tech debt in files TOUCHED by this story. Follow the tech debt discovery rules from ace-code-reviewer.md:
Only flag debt in EXISTING code, not new code (new code issues are blockers) Record: file, subsystem, description, severity, discovering story Severity: high (security/data loss), medium (quality/maintainability), low (cosmetic)
Output tech debt in YAML format as defined in ace-code-reviewer.md.

process.step:
Display the structured review report following ace-code-reviewer.md's report format:
``` ╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════╗ ║ ACE > Story Reviewed ║ ║ {INIT.story.id} "{INIT.story.title}" ║ ╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
{✓ or x} Review complete.
Status: {passed | issues_found} Blockers: {count} Warnings: {count} Files reviewed: {count} Artifacts verified: {passed}/{total} (3-level) AC coverage: {covered}/{total} scenarios Tech debt discovered: {count} items ```
Then display the full report sections: - Blockers list (if any) — file:line, description, severity, suggested fix - Warnings list (if any) — file:line, description, severity, suggested fix - Artifact verification matrix - AC coverage matrix - Tech debt YAML (if any)

success_criteria.criterion: Init function executed (environment detected, story validated)
Acceptance criteria present — STOPPED if missing
Technical solution present — STOPPED if missing
Story sections extracted (AC, Technical Solution, Out of Scope)
Review scope identified (changed files or technical solution artifacts)
3-level artifact verification completed for EVERY artifact in Technical Solution
Full review checklist executed (8 blocker checks, 3 warning checks)
Dead code detection run on all changed files
Stub detection run on all changed files
Coding standards compliance checked if standards available
AC coverage verified — every Gherkin scenario checked
Tech debt in existing code discovered and reported in YAML format
Structured report displayed following ace-code-reviewer report format
Completion banner with summary stats displayed
